message:
perf(fsci-arrayapi): replace per-element unravel/ravel with incremental coordinate advancement in broadcast

The broadcast inner loop previously called `unravel_index` and
`ravel_index` on every output element, each performing O(rank)
divisions and multiplications plus allocating a fresh Vec<usize>.
For large tensors this dominated wall-clock time in broadcast-heavy
conformance workloads (P2C-007 scenarios exercise creation + broadcast
as the first step in nearly every case).

Replace both helpers with:
- `row_major_strides(dims)`: precomputed once before the loop, returns
  a Vec of cumulative products so that a dot-product with coordinates
  gives the linear index — no division needed.
- `advance_row_major_coords(coords, dims)`: an odometer-style in-place
  increment that walks through output coordinates in row-major order,
  reusing a single mutable Vec across all iterations.

The broadcast loop now accumulates `in_linear` by summing
`coord * in_stride` per input dimension (skipping broadcast-1 dims),
entirely avoiding the separate ravel call. The coordinate vector is
advanced in-place only when there is a next iteration, removing the
need for unravel.

Net effect: broadcast goes from O(N * R) allocations + O(N * R)
divisions to O(1) allocation + O(N * R) additions, where N is the
output element count and R is the rank. This matters most for the
10k-element+ broadcast benchmarks added in the companion commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
